wiki_pages.markdown:
**Summary:** The ALL ALS Clinical Research Consortium is establishing research to collect a wide range of samples, clinical information and measurements from Amyotrophic Lateral Sclerosis (ALS) symptomatic, ALS gene carriers and control cohorts. This consortium is being funded by the National Institutes of Health/National Institute of Neurological Disorders and Stroke (NIH/NINDS) and managed by two clinical coordinating centers (CCC) at Barrow Neurological Institute and Massachusetts General Hospital. The clinical sites are distributed across the country, and led by a group of collaborative principal investigators. ASSESS ALL ALS is a prospective, observational cohort study enrolling individuals who are currently living with ALS (Symptomatic cohort) and individuals without ALS or known ALS gene mutations (Control cohort). Symptomatic cohort participants have options of both on-site and off-site/remote enrollment. The overarching goal of ASSESS ALL ALS is to prospectively collect, store and harmonize a large set of longitudinal data, which will be shared through the AMP ALS central portal, and biospecimens that will be linked to the clinical data for open science sharing with the research community. More information about the consortium and studies can be found at all-als.org **Overall Design:** In ASSESS study, the participants will be followed for 24 months (2 years), and will include collection of medical history, clinical outcomes, and blood samples once in 4 months for Symptomatic cohort and once in 12 months for Control cohort. Standard Clinical outcome measures for Symptomatic cohort include on-site assessments of handheld dynamometry (HHD) of 3 hand muscles bilaterally and vital capacity (VC), and both on-site and remote assessments of ALSFRS-R, Edinburgh Cognitive and Behavioral ALS Screen (ECAS), and Minimally Important Difference (MID) anchor questionnaires. Additionally, regular digital data collection of self-reported electronic Patient Reported Outcomes (ePROs) and speech measures were collected. The ePROs include ALS Functional Rating Scale-Revised Self entry (ALSFRS-RSE), ALS Impairment Multi-domain scale (AIMS), Environmental History and Social Determinants of Health (SDOH). Biospecimens include plasma, serum, RNA, whole blood for generation of induced pluripotent stem cells (iPSCs) and DNA for whole genome sequencing, and cerebrospinal fluid (CSF) via lumbar puncture (LP).

[**DOI:** 10.1002/mus.28244](https://doi.org/10.1002/mus.28244) **Note:** ALL ALS Consortium dataset content on the ALS Knowledge Portal is dynamic and may change over time. While best efforts are made to ensure data quality, this data is provided 'as is,' and changes are periodically released.  Forms with active queries are not transferred from the ALL ALS Data Coordination Center to the ALS KP and may appear in future releases. Please review dataset DOI numbers and timestamps when requesting, downloading, or analyzing data.
